text:
MAGNETIC RESONANCE IMAGING — NECK WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

EXAMINATION: MRI neck with and without gadolinium contrast
INDICATION: 52-year-old woman with newly diagnosed HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the left base of tongue (T2N1M0, Stage III). Pre-treatment planning evaluation. Assessment of primary tumor extent, deep tissue involvement, and regional nodal disease.
TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the neck performed on a 3.0 Tesla magnet. Sequences include sagittal T1, axial T1, axial T2 with fat saturation, axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) mapping, axial and coronal post-contrast T1 with fat saturation, and coronal STIR. Gadolinium-based contrast (gadobutrol 7.5 mL) administered intravenously without adverse reaction. Images reviewed in three planes.

COMPARISON: CT neck with contrast (obtained approximately 3 days prior).

FINDINGS:

PRIMARY TUMOR — OROPHARYNX / BASE OF TONGUE:
There is a well-defined mass arising from the left lateral aspect of the base of tongue. On T2-weighted sequences, the lesion demonstrates heterogeneous but predominantly T2-hyperintense signal relative to the adjacent tongue musculature. On DWI, there is conspicuous restricted diffusion (low ADC values estimated at approximately 0.78 x 10⁻³ mm²/s), consistent with densely cellular tumor. Following gadolinium administration, the mass shows brisk, avid, and heterogeneous enhancement. The lesion measures approximately 2.2 x 1.9 cm in the axial plane and 1.7 cm in the craniocaudal dimension.

The inferior margin of the lesion is at the level of the vallecula but does not extend into the vallecula or the pre-epiglottic fat space, which appears preserved. The lesion does not cross the midline; the right base of tongue, right lingual tonsil, and right lateral pharyngeal wall are all grossly normal in signal and enhancement pattern. Posteriorly, no clear invasion of the posterior pharyngeal wall musculature is identified. The superior margin of the lesion abuts but does not convincingly infiltrate the left tonsillar fossa; there is no discrete mass in the left tonsillar pillar or soft palate. The deep margin of the lesion shows irregular T2-hyperintense infiltration of the superficial fibers of the left hyoglossus muscle, but the genioglossus and intrinsic tongue musculature of the midline appear uninvolved. No involvement of the floor of mouth is identified. The mucosa overlying the lesion is thickened and irregular. There is no macroscopic erosion of the mandible or hyoid bone. The sublingual space and submandibular space are clear bilaterally.

REGIONAL LYMPH NODES:
Left neck: A single pathologically enlarged lymph node is present at the left level IIA station, measuring 2.1 x 1.8 cm in axial dimensions. The node demonstrates loss of the normal fatty hilum, irregular capsular margins, and internal signal heterogeneity on T2-weighted imaging with a focal T2-hyperintense, T1-hypointense center consistent with central necrosis. Post-contrast imaging confirms peripheral rim enhancement surrounding a non-enhancing necrotic core. The external capsule of the node appears intact on all sequences; the fat planes between the node and the adjacent sternocleidomastoid muscle are preserved. No discrete radiographic evidence of extranodal extension is identified, though the capsular margins are slightly irregular and warrants careful attention at surgical or radiation planning review.

No additional enlarged or morphologically abnormal lymph nodes are identified in the left level IB, III, IV, or V stations. No left retropharyngeal lymphadenopathy is identified.

Right neck: No pathologically enlarged or morphologically abnormal lymph nodes in any right-sided level. A few sub-centimeter nodes are noted in the right level II station, all with preserved fatty hila and no restricted diffusion; these appear reactive.

Retropharyngeal space: No enlarged retropharyngeal lymph nodes bilaterally.

SKULL BASE AND PARAPHARYNGEAL SPACE:
The skull base marrow signal is normal with no focal T1 hypointensity or T2 hyperintensity to suggest infiltration. Foramina of the skull base appear intact. The bilateral parapharyngeal fat spaces are preserved. The bilateral carotid arteries and internal jugular veins are patent, without encasement or displacement by tumor or adenopathy. The bilateral masticator spaces, parotid spaces, and pterygomandibular raphe are normal.

LARYNX AND HYPOPHARYNX:
The supraglottis, glottis, and subglottis are unremarkable. The epiglottis, aryepiglottic folds, and arytenoids are symmetric and normal in signal. No laryngeal or hypopharyngeal mass. The pyriform sinuses are symmetric and clear. The pre-epiglottic space contains normal fat signal.

SALIVARY GLANDS:
The bilateral parotid and submandibular glands are symmetric in size and signal intensity. No focal nodule or ductal obstruction.

VASCULAR STRUCTURES:
The bilateral common carotid arteries, internal and external carotid arteries, and internal jugular veins are patent and demonstrate normal flow voids. No vascular encasement.

VERTEBRAL COLUMN AND PREVERTEBRAL SPACE:
The cervical vertebral bodies demonstrate normal marrow signal without evidence of osseous involvement. Disc spaces are maintained. No prevertebral soft tissue thickening or signal abnormality.

THYROID GLAND:
The thyroid gland is normal in size and signal. No discrete nodule above the threshold of detection.

IMPRESSION:
1. Left base of tongue mass, measuring approximately 2.2 x 1.9 x 1.7 cm, with restricted diffusion and avid gadolinium enhancement, consistent with the known primary squamous cell carcinoma. The lesion is confined to the left lateral base of tongue with superficial involvement of the left hyoglossus fibers. No extension across the midline, no vallecula or pre-epiglottic space involvement, and no floor-of-mouth or mandibular involvement identified.
2. Single left level IIA cervical lymph node with central necrosis and peripheral rim enhancement, measuring 2.1 x 1.8 cm, consistent with metastatic nodal disease. The external capsule appears grossly intact without definitive extranodal extension on this examination, though irregular capsular contour is noted.
3. No contralateral cervical lymphadenopathy.
4. No involvement of the retropharyngeal nodes, skull base, carotid sheaths, or prevertebral space.
5. Overall findings are concordant with the staging CT and recent PET-CT; primary tumor remains confined to the left base of tongue with a single ipsilateral nodal metastasis.
